probability_composition.description: The short-term probability replaces FSRS-6, then a smoothstep blend returns to FSRS-6. Probabilities are not added, so evidence is not counted twice. The handover is routed on the previous review's outcome, per the predeclared, conservative re-route in ROUTING_DECISION_RULE.md (applied after the 201-300 transfer result): after a previous success, after no previous outcome at all, or on the first predictive review (the seed review's outcome is not treated as a genuine previous outcome for routing), full short-term weight holds only through 21,600 s (6 hours) and decays to 0 at 86,400 s (1 day) -- the routed model moves onto FSRS-6 much sooner than v2 here. After a previous failure, full short-term weight holds through 345,600 s (4 days) and decays to 0 at 432,000 s (5 days) -- the last bucket at or above 24-36 hours where the routed model won both metrics on both transfer cohorts, far less generous than v2's fitting-cohort boundary. Coefficients are unchanged from v1/v2; only these boundaries differ. FSRS-6 alone governs beyond 604,800 s on both paths.

supported_features.prior_counts: non-negative; each count is capped at 8 for the model feature
